/**
   * TOML related APIs
   */
  namespace TOML {
    /**
     * Parse a TOML string into a JavaScript object.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The TOML string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript object
     */
    export function parse(input: string): object;
  }

  /**
   * INI related APIs
   */
  namespace INI {
    /**
     * Parse an INI-formatted string into a JavaScript object.
     *
     * Supports:
     * - `; comment` and `# comment` full-line comments
     * - `[section]` headers (returned as nested objects)
     * - `key = value` and `key: value` pairs
     * - Quoted values (`"..."` or `'...'`)
     * - Inline comments after unquoted values
     * - Type coercion: booleans (`true`/`false`/`yes`/`no`/`on`/`off`),
     *   integers, and floats become the corresponding JS primitives.
     * - Duplicate `[section]` headers are merged (later keys win).
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The INI string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript object
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { INI } from "bun";
     *
     * const config = INI.parse(`
     *   ; this is a comment
     *   global_key = hello
     *
     *   [database]
     *   host = localhost
     *   port = 5432
     *   enabled = true
     * `);
     * // { global_key: "hello", database: { host: "localhost", port: 5432, enabled: true } }
     * ```
     */
    export function parse(input: string): Record<string, unknown>;

    /**
     * Convert a JavaScript object into an INI-formatted string.
     *
     * Rules:
     * - Root-level primitive properties (boolean, number, string, null) are
     *   emitted as `key=value` pairs before any section block.
     * - Root-level plain-object properties become `[key]` section blocks;
     *   their own primitive children are emitted as `key=value` pairs.
     * - Arrays, functions, symbols, and `undefined` values are skipped.
     * - String values that contain syntactically significant characters
     *   (`=`, `[`, `]`, `#`, `;`, newlines, etc.) or leading/trailing
     *   whitespace are automatically double-quoted.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The JavaScript object to serialize
     * @returns An INI-formatted string
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { INI } from "bun";
     *
     * const ini = INI.stringify({
     *   global_key: "hello",
     *   database: {
     *     host: "localhost",
     *     port: 5432,
     *     enabled: true,
     *   },
     * });
     * // global_key=hello
     * // [database]
     * // host=localhost
     * // port=5432
     * // enabled=true
     * ```
     */
    export function stringify(input: Record<string, unknown>): string;
  }

  /**
   * JSONC related APIs
   */
